name: Ermita de la Virgen de los Santos, Pico Tablado y Collado del Tablado – circular desde Borobia
region_intro: Hiking around Borobia offers diverse terrain, characterized by the foothills of the Moncayo mountain range and deep gorges. The region features a mix of open landscapes and forested areas, providing varied scenery for outdoor activities. Many circular hikes Borobia are available, allowing exploration of the natural park's features. These hiking trails around Borobia cater to different fitness levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Borobia The most popular hiking route is Moncayo from Cueva de Ágreda Trail, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 58 minutes to complete. This difficult route involves a significant ascent towards the Moncayo peak. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valcongosto Gorges and Cuartún - circular from Purujosa, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. This circular route explores the Valcongosto Gorges and the area around Cuartún. Local hikers also love the Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal from Calcena — circular by Penas del Cabo, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Borobia is defined by its gorges, hills, and proximity to the Moncayo Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging hikes near Borobia. The routes in Borobia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Borobia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Borobia?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Borobia, offering a variety of experiences from moderate walks to challenging ascents. The region's diverse terrain, characterized by the foothills of the Moncayo mountain range and deep gorges, provides ample opportunities for exploration.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Borobia?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, many of the moderate trails can be suitable for beginners with a reasonable fitness level. These paths often have less elevation gain and cover shorter distances, allowing for a more relaxed experience in the beautiful Borobia landscape.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Borobia?
A: Yes, Borobia offers several circular hiking routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971965058/valcongosto-gorges-and-cuartun-circular-from-purujosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorges and Cuartún - circular from Purujosa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.5-mile (7.3 km) path exploring the gorges. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971977216/mirador-de-la-ermita-de-san-cristobal-from-calcena-circular-by-penas-del-cabo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal from Calcena — circular by Penas del Cabo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.3 miles (8.5 km) and offers varied terrain with viewpoints.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails near Borobia?
A: The trails around Borobia offer access to impressive natural attractions. You can explore various caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to significant natural monuments, including the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Borobia?
A: Absolutely. Borobia is an excellent destination for experienced hikers seeking a challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057568211/moncayo-from-cueva-de-agreda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo from Cueva de Ágreda Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.4-mile (11.9 km) route with significant ascent towards the Moncayo peak. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971982775/calcena-trasobares-barranco-de-valdeplata-calcena-circular-by-the-moncayo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calcena - Trasobares - Barranco de Valdeplata - Calcena&lt;/a&gt; circular route, which spans over 16 miles (26 km) and involves substantial elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike around Borobia?
A: The best time to hike around Borobia is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Spring brings wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful fall colors. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear for colder temperatures and potential snow, particularly at higher altitudes in the Moncayo range.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Borobia?
A: The hiking trails in Borobia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views of the Moncayo mountain range, and the opportunity to explore deep gorges and forested areas. The variety of routes catering to different fitness levels is also frequently appreciated.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature river sources or unique geological formations?
A: Yes, you can find hikes that highlight unique natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059042862/cabezo-cuartal-y-nacimiento-del-rio-isuela-circular-desde-beraton-por-la-sierra-del-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo Cuartal y Nacimiento del río Isuela – circular desde Beratón por la Sierra del Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, takes you to the source of the Isuela River, offering a unique geological experience within the Sierra del Moncayo.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Borobia during spring?
A: For spring hiking in Borobia, it's best to dress in layers. Mornings can be cool, but temperatures often rise significantly during the day. A moisture-wicking base layer, a fleece or light insulated jacket, and a waterproof/windproof outer shell are recommended. Comfortable hiking boots with good ankle support are essential, as is sun protection (hat, sunglasses, sunscreen) and plenty of water.

Q: Are there any specific safety considerations for hiking in the Borobia region?
A: When hiking in Borobia, especially on more challenging routes or in remote areas, it's important to be prepared. Always carry enough water, snacks, and a fully charged phone. Inform someone of your route and expected return time. Weather conditions in mountainous areas like Moncayo can change quickly, so check forecasts before you go and be ready for variations. Some trails may have rocky or uneven terrain, so wear appropriate footwear.

Q: Can I find trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Many trails in the Borobia region provide excellent panoramic views, especially those ascending into the Moncayo foothills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971977216/mirador-de-la-ermita-de-san-cristobal-from-calcena-circular-by-penas-del-cabo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Ermita de San Cristobal from Calcena — circular by Penas del Cabo&lt;/a&gt; is specifically named for its viewpoint, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding landscape. Higher elevation routes, such as those leading towards the Moncayo Summit, also promise breathtaking panoramas.
tips: At this point we can take a detour to visit the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de Todos los Santos, an ideal place to take a break before continuing with our route.
From the top of the Tablado, at 1,747 meters above sea level and under the gaze of the wind mills, we have great views of Moncayo, Cabezo del Caiz and Almurdejos, among others.

Located 1365 meters above sea level, the Tablado pass offers fantastic views of the surrounding landscape. From here, you can descend to Purujosa or Borobia. An information panel at the pass provides more details about points of interest along your route.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of All Saints
